Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat(auth): services verify via JWKS, not a pinned static key (convergence B)

Every service verified user/service tokens with a static API_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY /
SERVICE_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY that did NOT match auth's actual signing key — so no token
verified in prod. Switch all services to verify against auth's JWKS endpoint by
key id (jose createRemoteJWKSet), the intended convergence design: key rotation
on auth just works, no consumer redeploy.

- server-kit: remoteJwks() + a VerifyKey type; the verifiers accept a JWKS
  resolver (verifyJwt branches the overload). newUserVerifier/newServiceVerifier
  unchanged for callers.
- menu-api, tutor-api verify USER tokens via remoteJwks(AUTH_JWKS_URL); audit,
  billing, email verify SERVICE tokens the same way. Static-key config removed.
- auth migrate: createDatabase like every other service (self-provision the DB).
- dev: compose.yaml runs the whole backend with hot reload (one-shot install +
  node --watch), scripts/dev-initdb.sql pre-creates dev DBs; `pnpm dev` = all.

Config alignment (issuer -> https://auth.iedora.com, audience -> iedora) + auth
realm/service-client seeding land alongside the deploy.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:

  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`

  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).

serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:

postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers

service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`

  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`

  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub

services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.

  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.
