Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.

  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:

  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

Prototype the long-term orchestration + linting backbone.

Turborepo:
- turbo.json defines typecheck/lint/test/build; `bun run typecheck|lint|test`
  now = `turbo run …`. Dependency-ordered, cached — a warm typecheck is
  `>>> FULL TURBO` in ~20ms vs ~2s cold.
- packageManager pinned (bun@1.3.3). .turbo gitignored (+ untracked a stray
  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback
  (`typecheck:parallel`).

oxlint pre-pass (the Vercel dual-linter pattern):
- .oxlintrc.json (correctness category). `lint` runs `oxlint && turbo run lint`
  — oxlint catches obvious bugs repo-wide in <1s, then ESLint runs the
  type-aware / react-hooks / boundaries rules it can't. Fixed the 5 issues
  oxlint's correctness pass found (useless spreads, prefer Set#size).

CI (was missing): deleting .forgejo/ in #121 removed the correctness CI with
no GitHub replacement. Recreated .github/workflows/ci.yml — path-filtered
correctness (typecheck+lint+test through turbo, .turbo cached across runs via
actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +
a ci-ok fan-in gate. Repointed the stale pre-commit actionlint hook
.gitea/ → .github/.

typecheck + lint + test green through the new pipeline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

build: Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass + restore correctness CI (#124)

* build: adopt Turborepo + oxlint pre-pass; restore correctness CI

  committed cache log). run-parallel.mjs kept as a no-cache fallback

actions/cache) + security (gitleaks + hadolint on both images + osv-scanner) +

.gitea/ → .github/.

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.

  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

.gitea/ → .github/.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* chore(security): refresh gitleaks baseline

Baseline was last generated 2026-06-22; the restored CI's gitleaks gate
flagged 8 findings committed after that (dev .env/compose placeholders,
old pre-consolidation dev/infra fixtures in history — all confirmed
non-production). Regenerated against current history: 57 → 61 entries,
`no leaks found` against the new baseline.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* build: make the oxlint+turbo setup production-real

Not a prototype anymore — the dual-linter is properly integrated, config is
inherited not copied, CI is remote-cache-ready, and the osv vulns it caught
are fixed.

Dual-linter done right (eslint-plugin-oxlint):
- @iedora/eslint-config now exports composed presets — /lib, /react-lib,
  /next-product — each ending with eslint-plugin-oxlint's disable layer
  (buildFromOxlintConfig, correctness), so ESLint no longer re-checks what the
  oxlint pre-pass already ran. Canonical placement: oxlint layer LAST.
- Every workspace inherits its preset in ONE line
  (`export { default } from '@iedora/eslint-config/lib'`) — 20 copied configs
  collapse to one source of truth; a package can only pick a flavor, not fork
  the rules. menu/web composes /next-product's pieces + its boundaries.

Turbo remote cache:
- CI correctness job takes TURBO_TOKEN/TURBO_TEAM/TURBO_API (secret + vars);
  stand up a self-hosted cache (ducktape / turborepo-remote-cache on homelab)
  and the whole team + CI share one cache. Empty = falls back to the
  actions/cache step, so CI works today with no extra infra.

Security (osv-scanner findings from the new CI):
- dropped the phantom `nodemailer` dep from @iedora/service-runtime (declared,
  never imported) — kills the nodemailer 6.10.1 advisories; only @iedora/service-email
  uses nodemailer, already on 9.x.
- pinned transitive js-yaml to ^4.3.0 via overrides (was 4.2.0 via @redocly).
- osv-scanner: "No issues found".

typecheck + lint + test green; ci.yml passes actionlint.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: provision test Postgres for the integration harnesses

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

The correctness job runs on GitHub-hosted runners with no ambient DB; the
billing/auth/server-kit harnesses createScratchDatabase() against a superuser
Postgres (ADMIN_URL / TEST_DATABASE_URL). Add a postgres:18-alpine service +
point TEST_DATABASE_URL at it. DATABASE_URL stays unset so the HAS_DB-gated
framework/db suites skip, matching local.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: serialize the test lane (single Postgres, scratch-DB harnesses)

Parallel test suites each CREATE a scratch DB on the one CI Postgres service;
running them concurrently overwhelmed the small service container (dropped
connections — service-auth passed, service-billing got Connection closed).
Run `turbo run test --concurrency=1`; scratch DBs already isolate data so
serial is correctness-neutral.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: disable SSL on the test Postgres DSN

bun 1.3.14 on the linux runner negotiates SSL against the no-SSL
postgres:alpine service and the connection closes; force sslmode=disable
(macOS/older-bun locally didn't hit this).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: pin bun to 1.3.3 (matches packageManager; 1.3.14 bun:sql closes conns)

The integration harnesses connect fine under bun 1.3.3 locally but got
ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner's 1.3.14 against the postgres
service. Pin CI to 1.3.3 to match the packageManager field.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: connect to test Postgres over 127.0.0.1; drop dead testcontainers deps

The scratch-DB harnesses got ERR_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_CLOSED on the runner:
bun resolves 'localhost' to IPv6 ::1, but the GitHub service container
publishes on IPv4 127.0.0.1 — a classic services gotcha. A CI-identical
postgres:18-alpine passes locally; force 127.0.0.1.

Also drop testcontainers + @testcontainers/postgresql from the catalog:
declared but never imported. testkit.ts deliberately avoids testcontainers
(hangs under Bun, testcontainers-node#974) and uses an ambient Postgres via
TEST_DATABASE_URL instead.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run test Postgres on the host network (bun:sql vs service NAT)

bun:sql's handshake (bun#16691) gets Connection closed through the GitHub
service-container NAT — the identical postgres:18-alpine + bun 1.3.3 connect
fine locally. Run Postgres via docker run --network host instead of a
services: block so it sits directly on the runner's localhost:5432, no NAT.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: add DB connectivity debug step (temporary diagnostic)

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci: run tests before typecheck/lint (Postgres starved under static-analysis load)

Diagnostic proved bun:sql connects fine right after install (psql -> PG 18.4,
bun:sql -> ok:1); the harnesses only got Connection closed once the heavy
tsgo+eslint sweep had run first. Run the DB test lane while the runner is idle,
then the CPU-heavy static analysis.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* ci(turbo): pass TEST_DATABASE_URL through to the test task

Root cause of the CI Connection-closed: Turbo strips undeclared env vars from
task subprocesses for cache determinism, so TEST_DATABASE_URL never reached
`bun test`. The harness then fell back to its default ADMIN_URL (:55433) —
present locally as my container, absent on the runner. Declare it in the test
task passThroughEnv. Restore the normal typecheck/lint/test order.

Description: Discovered a potential basic authorization token provided in a curl command, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat(auth): one unified-SSO auth platform + one @iedora/sdk (#118)

* feat(auth-sso): B0+B2 — make services/auth the standalone superset + shared-domain cookies

B0: replace the platform single-tenant auth (A) at services/auth with the
standalone multi-tenant OIDC auth (B) code (superset: oauth/orgs/OIDC-discovery/
manage). Rename @iedora/auth -> @iedora/service-auth, flip @iedora deps to
workspace:*, add types:[bun]. The one auth service going forward.

B2: add cookieDomain to AuthNextConfig, threaded through cookieOptions (used by
both the middleware refresh + server-action paths). Setting it to .iedora.com
lets every product subdomain share one session cookie = the basis for SSO.

Verified: 28 workspaces typecheck green. NOTE migrate entry is src/platform/
migrate.ts (shared Dockerfile entrypoint expects src/migrate.ts) — normalize in P5.

Part of the unified-SSO auth convergence (plan: jazzy-baking-peach).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* feat(auth-sso): B1 — seed script for the shared iedora realm

Idempotent scripts/seed-realm.ts: provisions the single iedora tenant (the shared
consumer directory) + password provider via the auth admin API, with
allowedOrigins for all *.iedora.com surfaces. All products authenticate against
this one realm (audience iedora, cookie on .iedora.com) = SSO.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* refactor(auth): one auth package — merge auth-sdk-nextjs into @iedora/auth-sdk

Three auth packages became one. auth-sdk-nextjs is folded into @iedora/auth-sdk
as the ./next subpath (createAuthNext + middleware + client), and a centralized
./next integration is added: authConfig/authNext/getAccount + login/register/
logout/forgot/reset, pre-wired for the ONE shared iedora realm (audience iedora,
SSO cookie on .iedora.com). Products import auth from @iedora/auth-sdk/next — no
separate nextjs package, no per-product createAuthNext. Repointed all consumers
(tutor, web) + package.json deps; deleted packages/sdk/auth-nextjs.

27 workspaces green. Product repoint onto the centralized integration (retiring
per-product auth wiring + menu api-client bespoke auth) is next.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* refactor(auth): centralize — menu + tutor use the one auth integration

Both products now import the session + operations from @iedora/auth-sdk/next
(getAccount, login/register/logout/forgot/reset) instead of wiring their own
createAuthNext or menu bespoke api-client auth. Deleted products/tutor/src/auth/
{config,index} (per-product realm); tutor auth/{session,actions} + vantage use the
centralized authNext/getClaims. Menu features/auth {actions,index} + account guard
rewritten onto the integration (SDK writes the SSO cookies; guards read verified
claims; menu keeps its tenantId=org vocabulary via a thin session map). Both
products now target the shared iedora realm — one account, SSO.

Added getAccessToken to the integration for the rare live-whoami (mcp) path.
Full monorepo typecheck green (0 failures). Only menu-fetch.test still touches
api-client ACCESS_COOKIE; api-client bespoke auth is otherwise unused (retire +
surface-auth.ts collapse + fetch-refresh rework are follow-ups).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* refactor(auth): collapse surface-auth to one realm + retire api-client bespoke auth

B5: apps/web/src/surface-auth.ts drops the per-surface AuthNextConfig (menu tenant
\"auth\" / tutor \"tutor\") — now just per-surface protectedPrefixes; proxy.ts runs
the ONE shared authConfig (moved to the edge-safe next/config, re-exported from
next/middleware) through resolveRefresh. One realm, one refresh, SSO.

Retire api-client bespoke auth: deleted auth-api/session/jwt/middleware/cookies/
config/refresh.test — api-client is now just ApiError + authedFetch (generic BFF
fetch). authedFetch refreshes via the centralized authClient.refresh + shared
cookie helpers (SSO-consistent domain cookie). Repointed the last consumers
(account actions → authClient.changePassword/listSessions/revokeSession; onboarding
→ createOrganization + authClient.refresh; my-devices → SessionView).

Full monorepo typecheck green (0 failures).

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

* refactor(sdk): one @iedora/sdk — merge audit/billing/email SDKs

Three SDK packages became one. @iedora/{audit,billing,email}-sdk fold into
@iedora/sdk as the ./audit, ./billing, ./email subpaths (packages/sdk/clients).
Zero-dep framework-agnostic clients, so the merge is clean. Repointed all ~42
consumers (@iedora/audit-sdk -> @iedora/sdk/audit, etc.) + 6 package.json deps
(deduped to one @iedora/sdk each). Deleted packages/sdk/{audit,billing,email}.

SDK package count: 5 -> 2 (@iedora/auth-sdk for the auth realm integration,
@iedora/sdk for the service clients). 25 workspaces typecheck green.

Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_0146BQCEQMYts9yTXEtd92V7

Description: Discovered a potential basic authorization token provided in a curl command, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat(auth-sso): B0+B2 — make services/auth the standalone superset + shared-domain cookies

B0: replace the platform single-tenant auth (A) at services/auth with the
standalone multi-tenant OIDC auth (B) code (superset: oauth/orgs/OIDC-discovery/
manage). Rename @iedora/auth -> @iedora/service-auth, flip @iedora deps to
workspace:*, add types:[bun]. The one auth service going forward.

B2: add cookieDomain to AuthNextConfig, threaded through cookieOptions (used by
both the middleware refresh + server-action paths). Setting it to .iedora.com
lets every product subdomain share one session cookie = the basis for SSO.

Verified: 28 workspaces typecheck green. NOTE migrate entry is src/platform/
migrate.ts (shared Dockerfile entrypoint expects src/migrate.ts) — normalize in P5.

Part of the unified-SSO auth convergence (plan: jazzy-baking-peach).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat: Bun/Hono/Kysely backend in the monorepo (phases 0–2: audit + auth)

Strangler migration of the Go backend → TypeScript (Bun + Hono + Kysely), unified
into this monorepo. Additive — the Next app + its deploy are unchanged.

Landed (17 bun tests green): monorepo (packages/server/*, services/*), OrbStack
dev compose, prod Bun Dockerfile; @iedora/contracts; @iedora/server-kit
(db/migrate/jose+JWKS/argon2/tokens/audit-outbox+relay/serviceauth/userauth/http/testkit);
audit service (Phase 1); auth service complete (Phase 2). Conventions in
services/AGENTS.md.

Remaining (follow-up PRs): admin BFF, billing, menu, frontend→contracts swap,
iedora-infra Kamal roles + the human-gated prod cutover. Backend tests aren't in
CI yet (need a Postgres service).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat(auth): port the auth service core to Bun/Hono/Kysely (vertical slices)

The core of Phase 2, built on server-kit and verified live in the OrbStack
compose stack. Vertical slices over the shared toolkit:

- migrations (users/tenants/memberships/sessions + outbox), copied verbatim from
  Go (idempotent → safe against the prod auth DB); types via kysely-codegen.
- data/: Kysely persistence — users (create/find/memberships/ban), sessions
  (insert / findByTokenHash / rotate with the atomic reuse-detection guard /
  revokeFamily / revokeAllForUser), tenants.
- session.ts: refresh-token sessions, rotation, access-token minting, the
  HttpOnly iedora_refresh cookie (Lax, /auth) — drop-in for the live frontend.
- features/{register,login,refresh,logout,jwks}: register (+auto-login), login
  (timing-equalized, ban check), refresh (rotation + family reuse-detection,
  rotate+audit in one tx), logout, and the EdDSA JWKS. Audit events flow through
  the OutboxWriter + relay into the audit DB.

Fixed: Bun's PostgresError carries the SQLSTATE in `errno` (not `code`) — used
for unique-violation 409 and the relay's transient/permanent classifier.

Verified: 6 auth tests (register, dup-409, login, refresh-rotate, reuse→401,
logout-revoke, JWKS) + live compose smoke (register/login/JWKS on :8180) + the
cross-service relay delivering auth events into audit_log. 14 backend tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Identified a Private Key, which may compromise cryptographic security and sensitive data encryption.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
ci: add audit workflow (gitleaks + hadolint + osv-scanner)

Three FREE, single-binary scanners pinned to specific versions
(supply-chain mitigation pós-Trivy compromise de Março 2026):

  gitleaks    v8.30.1  — secret regex scan (150+ patterns)
  hadolint    v2.14.0  — Dockerfile best practices
  osv-scanner v2.3.8   — npm/bun lockfile CVE scan vs OSV database

Single audit job, 3 tools sequenciais, ~30s total wall time (paraleliza
em capacity:3 com ci.yml, não tem critical path).

Caching pattern consistente com /var/cache/bun: binários vivem em
/var/cache/audit-tools (host bind-mount, whitelistado em runner config
valid_volumes). Install step é idempotent — re-runs típicos saltam os
3 curls.

Baselines + suppressions:
- .gitleaks-baseline.json (23 findings históricos pre-adoção: test
  fixtures em products/genkan removido, .env antigos, secrets em
  tfstate purged. CI bloqueia apenas leaks NOVOS).
- osv-scanner.toml (2 CVEs medium transitivos: esbuild via drizzle-kit
  e postcss via Next 16. Tracked upstream, rationale + ignoreUntil
  trimestral).

Hadolint corre com --failure-threshold error (3 warnings actuais são
pedantic ou exigem refactor; DL4006 é bug real e fica para corrigir).

Schedule: cron 0 6 * * 1 — Segunda 06:00 UTC pesca CVEs novos que
apareceram na OSV durante a semana mesmo sem push.

Também:
- .github/actionlint.yaml: whitelista label custom 'bun-debian' do
  self-hosted runner.
- scripts/git-hooks/pre-commit: passa -config-file explícito ao
  actionlint (auto-detect via .github/workflows/ falha porque
  migrámos para .gitea/workflows/ na deletação do GitHub Actions).
- runner valid_volumes: adicionado /var/cache/audit-tools.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat(menu): mobile-first dashboard + variant translations + promote-on-switch

- Restaurant home / theme / QR pages restructured around mobile-canonical action cards, sectioned forms, and the SectionHeader/Combobox/Checkbox primitives so every dashboard surface reads with the same vocabulary.
- Variant labels are i18n-aware end to end: schema (ItemVariant.labelI18n), Add/Edit dialog use a shared VariantsEditor, the public renderer localizes variant labels via the standard fallback chain, and AI Translate batches each variant label alongside name/description.
- Changing the restaurant's default language now atomically rotates every translatable surface (restaurant description, category name/description, item name/description, every variant label) inside one DB transaction. Theme editor surfaces a one-shot banner with rows-promoted + rows-needing-attention counts.
- DashboardPage gains a chrome="none" mode used by the restaurant home so menu / action cards claim the top of the viewport on mobile.
- New tests: 14 unit tests for promote helpers + 4 PGLite integration tests for the adapter transaction + 2 AI-translate variant cases. All 290 unit tests green.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat: zitadel-grant rename + Phase 2.5 grants-changed live session refresh

Two axes landing together:

── (1) cmd/zitadel-grant — pattern alignment ────────────────────────────────

Rename infra/cmd/zitadel-grant-iedora-admins → infra/cmd/zitadel-grant
(and the bin/ shim) to match the concise naming of the other cmds
(deploy, dev, doctor, with-secrets). Updated all callers:

  - infra/tofu/zitadel.tf       null_resource.iedora_admin_grants
  - infra/dev/tofu/main.tf      same, dev-side
  - .gitignore                  /infra/zitadel-grant build artefact

Added a root justfile recipe `just zitadel-grant *ARGS` so the cmd is
reachable from anywhere (matches `just with-secrets`). Tidied the
bin/zitadel-grant shim header.

Also expanded cmd/with-secrets/main.go package doc from 6 lines to a
proper Pipeline + Inputs + How-called + Failure-modes section so it's
on parity with cmd/iedora and cmd/zitadel-grant.

── (2) Phase 2.5 — grants-changed live session refresh ──────────────────────

New Zitadel Actions v2 EVENT target (`menu_grants`) that fires on every
`user.grant.*` event (added/changed/cascade.changed/removed/cascade.
removed/deactivated/reactivated) and POSTs to /api/zitadel/grants-changed
on the menu app. Lets active sessions refresh their resolved permission
set WITHOUT waiting for a re-login.

  - infra/tofu/zitadel.tf, infra/dev/tofu/main.tf
      zitadel_action_target.menu_grants + zitadel_action_execution_event
      for_each over the 7 event types (listed explicitly for auditability).
  - infra/modules/menu_env/main.tf
      New ZITADEL_GRANTS_SIGNING_KEY variable wired into env_map +
      secrets list. default = "" so rollout is optional (handler 503s
      when key is empty).
  - infra/tofu/containers.tf
      Wires the new signing key from the target into the prod menu_env
      module call.

Menu app:
  - New /api/zitadel/grants-changed route handler.
  - New zitadel-grants-webhook handler + tests.
  - New sessions feature slice (track active sessions for fan-out).
  - drizzle/0003 migration.
  - Admin sessions page at /dashboard/admin/sessions.
  - Updates to auth callback/logout + auth adapters (drizzle, session)
    to track session lifecycle.
  - Existing zitadel-webhook tests updated.

Trade-off: interrupt_on_error=false so a webhook outage doesn't block
grant writes — worst case is a stale session.row that the next login's
menu_permissions function target corrects.

Local reference checkouts moved out of git (services-references/).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat: QR sticker binding + Iedora-admin RBAC via Zitadel Actions v2

Restaurant QR stickers can be registered and bound to a restaurant on a
new admin surface at /dashboard/admin/qr-codes, gated by Zitadel project
roles. The iedora-admin role expands into atomic scopes (qr-codes:read/
write/update/delete) via an Actions v2 webhook at /api/zitadel/permissions
that is the single source of truth for bundle definitions across future
Iedora products. Webhook self-heals on first sign-in: when an admin
email signs in before any grant exists (the TF-side helper had no user
to target before Zitadel auto-provisioned), it POSTs the grant via the
mgmt API and includes the expanded scopes in the same append_claims
response so the first OIDC token already carries the right permissions.

Dev tooling: single Password1! for postgres + openobserve + OTel basic
auth; just dev --destroy now state-rm's zitadel_* before tofu destroy
to avoid AUTH-7fs1e from a stale JWT mid-teardown.

Investigation log: zitadel/zitadel#12185 (closed as not_planned —
false alarm, was actually a grant-timing issue on our side, now fixed
by the self-heal path above).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
feat: QR sticker binding + Iedora-admin RBAC via Zitadel Actions v2

Restaurant QR stickers can be registered and bound to a restaurant on a
new admin surface at /dashboard/admin/qr-codes, gated by Zitadel project
roles. The iedora-admin role expands into atomic scopes (qr-codes:read/
write/update/delete) via an Actions v2 webhook at /api/zitadel/permissions
that is the single source of truth for bundle definitions across future
Iedora products. Webhook self-heals on first sign-in: when an admin
email signs in before any grant exists (the TF-side helper had no user
to target before Zitadel auto-provisioned), it POSTs the grant via the
mgmt API and includes the expanded scopes in the same append_claims
response so the first OIDC token already carries the right permissions.

Dev tooling: single Password1! for postgres + openobserve + OTel basic
auth; just dev --destroy now state-rm's zitadel_* before tofu destroy
to avoid AUTH-7fs1e from a stale JWT mid-teardown.

Investigation log: zitadel/zitadel#12185 (closed as not_planned —
false alarm, was actually a grant-timing issue on our side, now fixed
by the self-heal path above).

Description: Identified a HashiCorp Terraform password field, risking unauthorized infrastructure configuration and security breaches.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
refactor(dev): drop docker-compose, drive entire dev stack via OpenTofu

Single declarative source dev + prod. `infra/modules/services/{postgres,
localstack,openobserve,zitadel,zitadel-login,house}/` define each
container; `infra/dev/tofu/main.tf` calls them with dev inputs
(local docker daemon, host-published ports, LocalStack standing in
for R2). Prod will pull in the same modules in a follow-up commit so
the container definitions stop existing in two DSLs.

What's gone:
- infra/dev/docker-compose.yml
- infra/dev/localstack/init.sh (script now inlined in the LocalStack
  module's `init_script` input)
- `docker compose up -d --wait` from dev.go
- compose-port lookups (replaced with plain `docker port`)

What's new:
- infra/modules/services/   six service modules + house (built locally
                            via docker_image with build {})
- dev.go uses two-pass `tofu apply`:
    1. -target=<docker resources> with empty PAT → containers up
    2. wait PAT file + /debug/ready → full apply with -var zitadel_pat
       seeds the project + OIDC app + emits the env files
- dev-down also cleans Docker leftovers by name (defensive against
  stale state from failed runs)

Verified from-scratch:
  just dev-down && just dev
  → 6 containers healthy, 19-key .env + 4-key .env.local emitted,
    house :3002/zitadel /debug/ready/openobserve /healthz all 200,
    summary surfaces canonical URLs read from .env + `docker port`.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: eduvhc
Message:
test(auth): un-skip OAuth handshake e2e + add role-escalation regression

Two missing security tests, both pinning behavior the audit register
already claims is mitigated:

**1. Real OAuth handshake e2e (`full-handshake.spec.ts`) is no longer skipped.**

The blocker was menu's `src/shared/brand.ts::GENKAN_URL` deriving from
`NODE_ENV` and pointing at production in the e2e build. brand.ts already
supported a `NEXT_PUBLIC_GENKAN_URL` override; nobody had wired it.
Plumbed through both ci.yml (job-level env, since the Next build is a
separate step in CI that inlines NEXT_PUBLIC_* at build time) and
playwright.config.ts (webServer env for local dev runs).

`oauth-flow.ts` also reshaped: the testkit doesn't serve Better Auth's
UI pages (login/signup), so the helper now signs up via the Better Auth
API directly (POST /api/auth/sign-up/email through the shim), seeds the
returned session cookie into the browser context for BOTH the shim AND
the underlying testkit origin (localhost vs 127.0.0.1 are different
hosts in cookie scope), then drives only the OAuth callback portion
through the browser. Still exercises every byte of menu's OAuth client
integration; just doesn't render genkan's signup form (which is genkan's
surface to test, not menu's).

**2. Role-escalation regression test (audit #20).**

`role-escalation.test.ts` pins the `additionalFields.role.input: false`
mitigation against the actual Better Auth API. Confirms the guarantee is
stronger than "field is silently dropped" — Better Auth REJECTS the
sign-up with `role is not allowed to be set`. A future BA upgrade that
relaxes this default would now fail loudly in CI.

Also exposed `@iedora/auth-testkit/schema` to consumers so genkan's test
can introspect `user.role` after signup without raw SQL.
